title: Re: New playthrough curiosity
description:
Lucky. Every time I do a runthrough with bugs is they show up in the worst time possible. It's either when there's another raid, or when everyone's sick. It's gotten to a point where I just place turrets in every large room that they might spawn in so I can have enough time to organize anyone capable of fighting back.

The bugs in caves are cool. I leave them alone. They're pretty good at dealing with raids.
label: impressionistic

title: Re: Huge VAC wave ongoing
description:
This has state sponsorship written all over it. Even saw some ads for CSGO/CS2 loot on Yandex. It would explain the proxies and great lengths they went to in order to obfuscate what's going on.

Seems some people owe me an apology after denying the cheating for profit problem …

Also, I would like to thank Support for taking my ticket about the VPN spam bots seriously.
label: analytical

title: Re: how do u guys beat bosses?
description:
I think you're supposed to hit them with the stick a bunch, but I only just started playing yesterday.

Elden Ring is the only particular Souls-like I've dove into, and frankly it IS an easy game if you take your time and actually use all the mechanics available to you. Every fight in the game can be cheesed multiple ways, even Malenia. You can shut down waterfowl dance with pots and not even bother learning how to dodge it reliably. If you want difficult, beat DMC3 on Dante-Must-Die mode with without using any of the infinite devil-mode costumes.
label: comparative

title: Re: Huge VAC wave ongoing
description:
22 000 is a small number considering how often cheating occurs. There is still nothing done to improve anti cheat into being more reliant. The vac coins would had help a lot into punishing certain knewed cheaters that expose themself... Would be curious what would were the numbers of cheaters banned when there would had been such a feature..

Considering that 22k is in a month... At 1.16 million players actively every hour... That number is like a shame.. It would mean that 12x 22k = 264 000 vac bans a year, that is still not explaining why the cheating behaviours not feel punished as there is always a cheater returning and cheating more, the length those people keep cheat and keep have satisfaction to play 300 hours by cheats, farming on multiple accounts cases that shows the problem.

title: Re: attracting young pawns?
description:
It's under "fertility procedures" research, and only requires basic herbal medicine as a minimum.

Yeah, it is a bit down the line of development, but you have time if it is a long term colony. Raising children is also quite difficult, as they take a lot of time, resources and provide little until they are older which takes ~3 ingame years normally. If you throw them in a vat the entire time it will be less than a year, but they will be far less developed.
label: analytical

title: Re: New playthrough curiosity
description:
Go to the workshop > find the setting to search for scenarios > browse the hundreds, if not thousands, of custom scenarios people have made.

I like doing random different starts, some with different bonuses, or different drawbacks. I also like seeing what people have cooked up.

For example there was one where you started out with like 30 thousand steel near you. Makes a lot of things easier, but also spikes your colony wealth so you better be prepared to handle larger than normal raids from the start.
label: analytical

title: Re: New playthrough curiosity
description:
I had actually considered back then over tuning my settings and scenarios but never really had the skill to keep a colony intact after it grows to a pretty good size since I always get wrecked by (for me) impossible odds that have me start all over again. Especially if I've spent hours trying to get the scenario right for something similar to an all out war with the inhabitants of the rimworld by pre preparing multiple outposts to keep the materials for the war effort flowing to having a pre built base only for the storyteller to suddenly send in a mechanoid threat the effectively tore than my base in minutes due to sheer bad luck of me mismanaging my pawns schedules and some of them breaking down in combat.

But yeah, I do understand the feeling of getting tired of the same random back to basics start, so every now and then I attempt to change things up, even with just small tweaks to the settings.
label: impressionistic
